prerequisites: An Asana account with access to the workspace or organization whose data you want to sync.
Decide on an authentication method: a Personal Access Token (single user) or OAuth 2.0 (for apps where multiple users sign in).
For OAuth 2.0: a registered Asana app in the developer console to obtain a client ID, client secret, and refresh token.
Optional: the organization export IDs (gid) for any workspaces or organizations you want to scope the sync to, supplied via the organization_export_ids config field.

authMethods.name: Personal Access Token (PAT)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Personal Access Token. Generate one in the Asana developer console at https://app.asana.com/0/my-apps and supply it as credentials.personal_access_token. A PAT grants the same access in the API as the user who created it.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Authenticate using the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow, used when multiple users need to sign in. Register an app in the developer console to obtain a client ID and client secret (credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret), then exchange an authorization code for an access token and a refresh token. Supply the client ID, client secret, and refresh token (credentials.refresh_token); the connector uses the refresh token to obtain new access tokens.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication method
setupSteps.body: Select Personal Access Token authentication for a single-user setup, or OAuth 2.0 if your integration must support multiple Asana users signing in. Both methods are configured under the credentials field.

setupSteps.title: Create a Personal Access Token (PAT option)
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Asana and open the developer console at https://app.asana.com/0/my-apps, then create a new Personal Access Token. Copy the token and provide it as credentials.personal_access_token. The token inherits the same Asana permissions as the user who generated it.

setupSteps.title: Register an OAuth app (OAuth option)
setupSteps.body: In the Asana developer console (https://app.asana.com/0/my-apps), create a new app and provide an app name, a redirect URL, and the OAuth permission scopes. Asana issues a client ID and client secret; keep the client secret private. Complete the authorization code flow to obtain a refresh token, then provide credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.refresh_token.

setupSteps.title: Scope and tune the sync
setupSteps.body: Optionally set organization_export_ids to limit the sync to specific Asana workspaces or organizations, adjust num_workers to control concurrency, and leave test_mode at its default unless you are testing. Run a connection check to confirm the credentials are valid.
